title: Kimchi Fried Rice
summary: Kimchi is king. If cooking veggie, check your kimchi to make sure it doesn’t contain shrimp. Go bold, the more potent the kimchi – the better the rice.

ingredients.label: 2 Tbsp Mixed Black & White Sesame Seeds
ingredients.name: Black And White Sesame Seed

method.description:
Slice the pak choi, keeping the white stalks and leaves separate,
and set aside.

Get a large frying pan or wok over a high heat. Drizzle in some
sesame oil and use a fork to add the kimchi straight from the jar,
(saving the juice for later) and the white pak choi stalks. Stir-fry for 1 minute, then tip in the pouches of rice. Use the back of your
spoon to break up the rice grains and give everything a good stir.
Turn down the heat to medium.

Get a second large frying pan over a high heat and drizzle in
a good glug of sesame oil. Crack in the eggs. Once the whites
begin to set, sprinkle over the sesame seeds and chilli flakes, then
use a spoon to baste the egg whites with the sesame oil until
they begin to crisp. Turn off the heat.

Back to the rice. Turn the heat back up and then stir through a
large splash of kimchi juice and the pak choi leaves. Tear in most
of the coriander leaves. Cook for a further minute.

Divide the kimchi fried rice between four bowls. Top each portion
with a sesame and chilli fried egg and tear over the remaining
coriander leaves to serve.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
